# Copilot Instructions — Haskell Learning Curriculum

## Curriculum Overview

This repository is a structured Haskell learning curriculum with 30 exercises in `exercises/NN-slug/`. Each exercise is a standalone Stack project. **The learner's only job is to edit `src/Exercise.hs`** — replacing `undefined` stubs with working implementations until `stack test` passes.

## Exercise Structure

```
exercises/NN-slug/
├── README.md              # Theory block + numbered practice assignments
├── stack.yaml             # Stack config — resolver: lts-22.39 (do not change)
├── package.yaml           # hpack config
├── src/
│   └── Exercise.hs        # Skeleton — learner fills in undefined stubs
└── test/
    ├── Spec.hs            # {-# OPTIONS_GHC -F -pgmF hspec-discover #-}
    └── ExerciseSpec.hs    # Pre-written Hspec tests — DO NOT MODIFY
```

## Critical Rules

- **Never modify `test/ExerciseSpec.hs`** — tests are the ground truth
- **Never change `resolver:` in `stack.yaml`** — all exercises use `lts-22.39` (GHC 9.6)
- **Never use partial functions** in skeletons: no `head`, `tail`, `fromJust` without a comment warning
- `src/Exercise.hs` must export every symbol imported by `ExerciseSpec.hs` — even as `undefined`
- Hspec `describe`/`it` strings in tests must exactly match assignment names in README.md
- README theory blocks may reference concepts from prior exercises only

## Build & Test

```bash
# Enter an exercise and run its tests
cd exercises/01-hello-haskell
stack test

# Run a single test group by name pattern
stack test --test-arguments "--match Assignment"

# Watch mode (rebuilds on file change)
stack build --file-watch

# Open a REPL for the exercise
stack ghci src/Exercise.hs

# Run all exercises
for dir in exercises/*/; do (cd "$dir" && stack test --silent); done
```

## Linting & Formatting

```bash
hlint src/
fourmolu --mode inplace src/
```

## Creating a New Exercise

1. `stack.yaml`: `resolver: lts-22.39`, `packages: [.]`
2. `package.yaml`: name `exercise-NN-slug`, expose `Exercise` module, test deps: `hspec >= 2.11`, `hspec-discover`, `QuickCheck`
3. `test/Spec.hs`: exactly `{-# OPTIONS_GHC -F -pgmF hspec-discover #-}`
4. `src/Exercise.hs`: type signatures + `= undefined` bodies with `-- | description` Haddock
5. `test/ExerciseSpec.hs`: `describe "Assignment N: <name>"` blocks; must compile against the skeleton
6. `README.md`: theory first (200–600 words, code examples showing concepts not answers), then `## Practice Assignments` with numbered sections **exactly matching** the test `describe` strings

## Key Haskell Conventions

- Module naming mirrors directory structure: `src/Foo/Bar.hs` → `module Foo.Bar`
- Prefer explicit export lists in every module
- Use `ExceptT`/`Either` for recoverable errors
- Declare `{-# LANGUAGE ... #-}` per file or via `default-extensions` in `package.yaml`
- Qualified imports for collision-prone packages: `Data.Map.Strict as Map`, `Data.Set as Set`, `Data.Text as T`
- Prefer `Data.Map.Strict` over lazy `Data.Map`
- `package.yaml` is the source of truth; the `.cabal` file is generated by hpack — do not edit it manually
